---
title: "Triticum Tale"
---

::: {.hero}
# Decode Durum Resilience

**Triticum Tale** transforms multi-omic stress data into a clear scientific narrative of how *Triticum durum* adapts under heat and drought pressure.

[Explore Meta-QTL Explorer](meta-qtl-explorer.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

Welcome to **Triticum Tale**, a digital museum for multi-omic stress data in *Triticum durum*.

This project brings together transcriptomic, metabolomic, and integrative analyses to explore how durum wheat responds to heat and drought stress through interactive, narrative-driven visualizations.

## Explore the story

Below is an interactive Plotly visualization that showcases simulated stress-response separation.

```{r}
#| label: fig-mock-plotly
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

library(tidyverse)
library(plotly)

set.seed(31)
n_per_group <- 12

pca_data <- bind_rows(
  tibble(
    Sample = paste0("Control_", seq_len(n_per_group)),
    PC1 = rnorm(n_per_group, mean = -2.5, sd = 0.6),
    PC2 = rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 0.8, sd = 0.5),
    Treatment = "Control"
  ),
  tibble(
    Sample = paste0("Heat_", seq_len(n_per_group)),
    PC1 = rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 2.2, sd = 0.7),
    PC2 = rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 1.9, sd = 0.6),
    Treatment = "Heat Stress"
  ),
  tibble(
    Sample = paste0("Drought_", seq_len(n_per_group)),
    PC1 = rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 1.1, sd = 0.7),
    PC2 = rnorm(n_per_group, mean = -2.3, sd = 0.6),
    Treatment = "Drought Stress"
  )
) %>%
  mutate(Treatment = factor(Treatment, levels = c("Control", "Heat Stress", "Drought Stress")))

plot_ly(
  data = pca_data,
  x = ~PC1,
  y = ~PC2,
  color = ~Treatment,
  colors = c("Control" = "#003b71", "Heat Stress" = "#c5a059", "Drought Stress" = "#8c2d19"),
  type = "scatter",
  mode = "markers",
  text = ~paste("Sample:", Sample, "<br>Treatment:", Treatment),
  hoverinfo = "text"
) %>%
  layout(
    title = "Simulated Multi-Omic PCA: Treatment Separation",
    xaxis = list(title = "PC1 (42%)"),
    yaxis = list(title = "PC2 (15%)")
  )
```

## Reproducibility Bridge

::: {.text-center .mt-4 .mb-5}
<a href="https://github.com/PALP31/BioAgro-Stats" class="btn btn-outline-primary" aria-label="Ver Pipeline Estadístico en GitHub">📊 Ver Pipeline Estadístico en GitHub</a>

*Todas las visualizaciones están respaldadas por modelos lineales mixtos y análisis multi-ómicos reproducibles.*
:::
